description.en: Maximum allowed back-reference distance the compressor can use when compressing data, expressed as power of two, 1 << window_log bytes. This parameter greatly influences the memory usage of compression. Higher values require more memory but gain better compression values. A value of zero causes the value to be selected automatically.

description.en: The base-two logarithm of the maximum size of the window used during decompression. This can be useful to limit the amount of memory used when decompressing data. A larger maximum window size leads to faster decompression. A value of zero causes the value to be selected automatically.
